args.code:
import pandas as pd

business_data = pd.DataFrame(locals()['var_function-call-5459169753406917344'])
review_data = pd.DataFrame(locals()['var_function-call-15463608801344723929'])

# Convert rating to numeric, coercing errors to NaN
review_data['rating'] = pd.to_numeric(review_data['rating'], errors='coerce')

# Drop rows where rating is NaN (if any)
review_data.dropna(subset=['rating'], inplace=True)

# Merge the two dataframes
merged_data = pd.merge(business_data, review_data, on='gmap_id')

# Calculate average rating for each business
average_ratings = merged_data.groupby('name')['rating'].mean().reset_index()

# Sort by average rating in descending order and get the top 5
top_5_businesses = average_ratings.sort_values(by='rating', ascending=False).head(5)

# Format the result
result = top_5_businesses.to_json(orient='records')

print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

review_data = pd.DataFrame(locals()['var_function-call-15463608801344723929'])

# Convert rating to numeric, coercing errors to NaN
review_data['rating'] = pd.to_numeric(review_data['rating'], errors='coerce')

# Drop rows where rating is NaN (if any)
review_data.dropna(subset=['rating'], inplace=True)

# Merge the two dataframes
merged_data = pd.merge(business_data, review_data, on='gmap_id')

# Calculate average rating for each business
average_ratings = merged_data.groupby('name')['rating'].mean().reset_index()

# Sort by average rating in descending order and get the top 5
top_5_businesses = average_ratings.sort_values(by='rating', ascending=False).head(5)

# Format the result
result = top_5_businesses.to_json(orient='records')

print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)
